subject: Marginal / no resolution improvement with M
body:
Hi guys, 

I am refining a tomo dataset of ~5000 particles from 109 tilt series of a 
somewhat flexible protein bound to some very heterogeneous additional 
density with Relion and M.  Data was collected on a K3, dose-symmetric tilt 
scheme, 120 e/Å total dose 2 degrees increment, 6 frames/movie (~0.3 
e/A/frame). The sample was purified, so we have thin ice. We didn't have 
fiducials, so I used only patch tracking to align the tilt series - this 
seemed to have worked reasonably well because particles are huge and have 
good contrast (mean residual error from etomo log files is 1.45 Å). 
Subtomos are at a pixel size of 5Å/px, original data was at 1.38Å/px. I 
started with ~20Å resolution when I imported the data into M,  then did the 
following refinements

   - First Refinement only poses and image warp grid with 3 x 3, one 
   subiteration. "Improved" from 20.0Å to 19.8Å
   - Second refinement with Image warp grid 9x6, Particle poses, Stage 
   angles, Volume warp grid 4x6x2x10. This run crashed, so I tried again the 
   same but without stage angles --> no improvement
   - Next, only particle poses and Stage angles --> no improvement
   - Next, again Image Warp with 6x4 & particle Poses --> No improvement

I am new to this, so I have no idea what/how much resolution improvement 
can be expected with this number of particles and parameters? ANy advice 
would be much appreciated!

Thanks a lot,
Matthias
